medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Patient 1 received treatment beginning at 1 month of age. She had two episodes of acute illness followed by seizure before reaching 1 year of age. Although there was no acute deterioration of motor ability after these episodes, her development was slightly delayed. Seizure-like movement had been observed at the age of 10 months after vaccination, and the patient was then prescribed phenobarbital which was later discontinued at 5 years of age. Currently, she exhibits no abnormal neurological signs.
medical_history.past_medical_history: Glutaric aciduria type I (GA-I), treated with special diets, carnitine supplements, and immediate stress avoidance.

physical_examination.specialty_examination: Brain MRI T2-weighted images revealing high signal intensity over the globus pallidus were observed at 1.3 years. The pallidal lesions resolved gradually, and disappeared after 6 years. High intensity over deep white matter was more prominent at older ages.

laboratory_examinations.changes_over_time: Mean DBS C5DC levels were elevated (mean 0.884, range 0.08 to 2.64 μM), except for Patient 4 who is a low excretor

laboratory_examinations.findings: Plasma lysine (mean 110.5, range 32.70 to 263.0 μM) and tryptophan (mean 50.28, range 12.30 to 100.0 μM) levels were kept near the lower limits of the normal ranges
Elevated DBS C5DC levels (mean 0.884, range 0.08 to 2.64 μM)
IVS10-2A>C mutation represented 50% of the mutated chromosomes; other mutations were all private

radiographic_examinations.findings: High signal intensity over the globus pallidus
radiographic_examinations.changes_over_time: Pallidal lesions resolved gradually and disappeared after 6 years; high intensity over deep white matter was more prominent at older ages

radiographic_examinations.findings: High density lesions over both the putamen and globus pallidus
radiographic_examinations.changes_over_time: Lesions resolved gradually, and at 4 years of age involved only the outer regions of the putamen; frontotemporal atrophy was still more prominent than that of other patients

radiographic_examinations.findings: Widening sylvian fissures and delays in myelination
radiographic_examinations.changes_over_time: At 3.9 years, improvement in brain atrophy but mildly increased intensity of deep white matter

radiographic_examinations.findings: Frontotemporal atrophy and delay in myelination
radiographic_examinations.changes_over_time: At age 3.4 years, frontotemporal atrophy had resolved, although high intensity of deep white matter had become apparent

radiographic_examinations.date: At ages more than 3 years (Patients 5 and 6)
radiographic_examinations.findings: High intensity in deep white matter

other_tests.procedure_name: Wechsler Preschool and Primary Scale of Intelligence, Revised Edition (WPPSI-R)
other_tests.findings: FIQ, PIQ, and VIQ scores ranged between 66 and 102

other_tests.procedure_name: Bayley Scales of Infant and Toddler Development, Third Edition (Bayley-III)
other_tests.findings: DQ estimated as 38% for cognition, 52% for receptive communication, 69% for expressive communication, 13% for fine motor, and 19% for gross motor
